title: fraxinus latifolia
intro: Fraxinus latifolia, also known as the Oregon ash or Oregon white ash, is a deciduous tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, characterized by its broad, green leaves and white or gray bark. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus latifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe skin irritations and reduce fever, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Fraxinus latifolia can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, alleviate pain, and support overall well-being.
